text:
Eurosport has taken swift action by removing commentator Bob Ballard from their Paris Olympics coverage following an "inappropriate" comment made during the broadcast.

The controversy erupted after Eurosport aired the women's 4x100 relay on Saturday, where the Australian team clinched gold. As the victorious swimmers Mollie O'Callaghan, Shayna Jack, Emma McKeon, and Meg Harris exited the Paris Aquatic Centre, Ballard's attempt at humour fell flat with a sexist remark.

"Well, the women (are) just finishing up. You know what women are like... hanging around, doing their make-up," said Ballard, which was captured during the live coverage of the event. The clip quickly circulated on social media platforms, prompting Eurosport to release a statement addressing the incident, reportsthe Mirror.

READ MORE:Adam Peaty - The rise, fall and rise again of 'The Beast' as Olympic immortality awaits in Paris

READ MORE:Adam Peaty's net worth is dwarfed by girlfriend's famous and wealthy fatherTeam GB's Adam Peaty is highly rated at this year's Olympics but he has a significantly smaller net worth than his girlfriend's father

"During a segment of Eurosport's coverage last night, commentator Bob Ballard made an inappropriate comment," the statement confirmed. "To that end, he has been removed from our commentary roster with immediate effect."

Ballard had been providing commentary alongside Lizzie Simmonds, who immediately branded his comment as "outrageous". Meanwhile, viewers expressed their disapproval on social media, with one user urging Ballard to "Bob Ballard hang your head in shame and get off the broadcast and back to the 1930s," Another lamented the shameful nature of the remarks, questioning the lack of training for such individuals: "When will these people ever learn? Why aren't they being given some training?"

"Eurosport please sack Bob Ballard from commentating any more at the Olympics 2024," demanded one viewer, appalled by his remarks about Australia's female 4x100m relay team, calling for a public apology due to the comments being "His comments re Aus 4x100 female swim relay team totally disgusting in today's world. He needs to publicly apologise!!!"

The Australian women's squad clinched the gold medal with a lead of over a second in their race. The US team secured the silver, while China was awarded the bronze.

Great Britain's team, featuring Anna Hopkin, Eva Oparo, Lucy Hope, and Freya Anderson, competed in the final but did not manage to secure a podium finish, ending up seventh, just behind the host nation France.

title: Olympics commentator removed from coverage after 'outrageous' comment - Stoke-on-Trent Live
author: Hayley Parker
description: Eurosport commentator Bob Ballard has been removed from the channel's coverage of the Paris Olympics after making a comment during the swimming
